page: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a high-memory instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 448 dedicated vCPUs derived from 224 physical cores of Intel Xeon 8176M processors running at 2.1 GHz. The system is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 6144.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, translating to 13.71 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but provides a high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth. The instance achieves top-tier ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests, reaching 18894.65 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 810.78 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, processing 297.29 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 100.48 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more modest, with Geekbench single-core and PassMark memory scores falling into the weaker percentiles.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the u-6tb1.112xlarge represents an ultra-dense hardware class designed to consolidate massive datasets into a single memory space. While the lack of bundled local storage requires external network-attached storage solutions, the high-density DDR4 memory and 100 Gbps network baseline minimize network bottlenecks for distributed data access. The trade-off lies in its single-threaded performance and memory latency scores, which are lower relative to smaller, higher-clocked instances.

This instance is engineered for massive in-memory databases, large-scale enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing workloads. Its high memory capacity and strong multi-core throughput make it highly suitable for hosting large SAP HANA deployments, real-time analytics engines, and complex scientific simulations that require keeping multi-terabyte datasets entirely in system memory.
description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is an ultra-high-memory instance designed for massive in-memory workloads. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 448 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8176M processors and 6144.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, providing a massive 13.71 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads reaching 810.78 GB/sec, alongside top-tier Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance and memory latency benchmarks are relatively weak. Qualitatively, this instance offers extreme resource density for consolidating massive datasets, making it highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive real-time analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge is a high-memory instance featuring 448 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 6 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services u-6tb1.112xlarge features 448 vCPUs, 6 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, built for massive in-memory database workloads.
tagline: An ultra-high-memory instance delivering six terabytes of RAM and massive multi-core compute for enterprise-scale in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with 448 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 6144 GB of DDR4 RAM.
Delivers 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-speed data transfer.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile.
Best suited for massive in-memory databases and large-scale enterprise applications.
